$AVGO helps hyperscalers build the custom chips and networking fabric needed to run massive AI systems.

If $GOOGL, $META, OpenAI or Anthropic want to build their own inference chips then Broadcom is one of the first calls because it has the IP, packaging expertise and networking portfolio required to scale custom AI systems.

Thats why Broadcom remains one of the strongest infrastructure positions in the AI economy.

$AVGO Semiconductor Solutions revenue grew 79% YoY as it becomes the design partner for hyperscalers building custom AI inference chips. 

$GOOGL, $META, OpenAI & Anthropic all want more of the chip layer for AI inference with Broadcom positioned as enabler behind that shift.
TEXAS AG LAUNCHES PROBE INTO $CELH ENERGY DRINKS

$HOOD launched 2026 World Cup prediction markets.

The product covers tournament winners, match results and player outcomes as prediction markets become one of Robinhood’s fastest-growing businesses.

Goldman Sachs projects SpaceX AI revenue could grow 100x to $322B by 2030 making AI the core case behind its reported $1.8T IPO valuation.

The forecast assumes total revenue reaches $474B by 2030 with AI becoming the largest segment ahead of Starlink and launch.
$AVGO said the company entered an agreement to give Anthropic access to another 5GW of next-generation $GOOGL TPU-based compute starting in 2027.

Honeywell-backed $QNT is going public today at a $16B valuation in the largest quantum IPO to date.

Polymarket gives Quantinuum a 36% chance of closing IPO day between a $22B and $25B market cap.

$IBM and $GOOGL Cloud launched a new practice to help enterprises deploy AI agents across hybrid systems.

IBM will use Google Cloud-certified consultants to build industry-specific agents for banking, government, retail, telecom, energy and life sciences.

$SOFI users can now sign up for a chance to buy SpaceX IPO shares before the expected June 12 debut.

Active Investing users can submit non-binding interest now and confirm orders before listing with limited allocations not guaranteed.

$AMZN unveiled Vulcan which is its first robot with a sense of touch built to pick and stow items in cramped fulfillment bins without damaging products.

The robot can handle ~75% of Amazon’s item types and will begin deploying across the U.S. and Europe over the next couple of years.

You can watch my full $TE breakdown here:

piped.video/91iawV0FTXw?si=eYUt…

$TSM CEO warned chip supply will remain short of AI demand for year with U.S. customers still exceeding available capacity.

TSMC reiterated 30%+ sales growth this year and said capex should trend toward high end of its $56B range while avoiding abrupt memory-style price hikes.

$TE wants to rebuild more of the U.S. clean energy supply chain onshore by starting with solar module manufacturing and expanding into battery storage through the $32M KORE Power acquisition.

T1 Energy is trying to position itself as a U.S.-based energy infrastructure supplier across both solar and storage.

$AAPL will reportedly use $GOOGL Cloud's $NVDA Blackwell fleet to power its overhauled Siri after its own Mac-chip servers proved too slow to run the model.

Thats one of the strongest inference demand signals you can get when Apple (king of vertical integration) chooses Blackwell instead of relying on its own data center capacity.

$AMZN is expanding robotics and faster delivery with new Proteus robots, STARK automation and Amazon Now 30-minute delivery expanding internationally.

It's also investing ~$11B+ to modernize European fulfillment and commit $1B to worker training by 2030.

📺 IS THIS A ROUTINE PULLBACK OR TREND CHANGE? + $TSLA ONE MORE CHANCE TO PROVE IT

After nearly eight weeks of relentless upside, the market is finally showing signs of fatigue.

The first cracks actually appeared when $GOOGL failed at the top of its range, and $AMZN  triggered a Red Dog Reversal sell signal. 

Those leadership breakdowns were enough to make me reduce risk, cut my long exposure from roughly 15 positions to just five, sell premium, and buy puts for the first time in weeks.

*

The key question now is whether this is simply a healthy pullback within a strong uptrend or the beginning of a larger trend change.

Both $SPY and $QQQ are opening below important short-term support levels and testing their 8-day after spending weeks above them. 

While many traders have been calling for a 3-5% correction, the market has only experienced about a day and a half of weakness so far. 

A move down toward the 21-day would still be completely normal after such a powerful advance.

*

What concerns most is the deterioration in leadership. 

$GOOGL broke down from a key area. $AMZN reversal signal was followed by a gap-down that never filled. $NVDA delivered a strong breakout above prior highs but failed to hold the move, triggering an adjustment point for active traders. 

When leading stocks stop acting like leaders, risk management becomes more important than aggressive buying.

*

Despite the weakness, I am not turning outright bearish. My focus is on staying "fine and flexible"—protecting gains, reducing exposure, and allowing price action to determine the next move. 

After reaching my new highs for the year earlier this week, I'm prioritizing capital preservation over forcing trades.

*

$TSLA remains on my watchlist.

While it showed some relative strength recently, the stock has become increasingly indecisive and is sitting near critical support around the $413-$416 area. 

I am willing to give #Tesla one more chance to prove itself and move back toward leadership status. 

If those levels fail, however, #TSLA could slide further down the watchlist as traders look elsewhere for stronger opportunities.

*

For now, the market finally looks vulnerable, leadership is weakening, and flexibility matters more than conviction. 

The next few sessions should reveal whether this is merely a routine pullback or the start of something larger.

*
